Dr. Roger Brent

personal site

Associate Director of Research. He received a B.A. in Computer Science and Mathematics from the University of Southern Mississippi and a Ph.D. in Biochemistry and Molecular Biology from Harvard University. Prior to joining the Institute, Dr. Brent was an Associate Professor at Harvard Medical School and Massachusetts General Hospital in Boston. Dr. Brent is known for his work on eukaryotic transcription regulation and its extension into biological technologies to map genetic networks and test functions of genes and alleles. He has also worked to generate computational tools to aid in understanding this data. Recently, Dr. Brent has begun to develop other methods to give greater insight into the flow of information within cells, and to measure the outcomes of perturbations of regulatory networks on the behavior of large numbers of individual cells. This research is likely to change the way researchers test ideas about gene function and lead to a broader understanding of the function of variation in protein sequence within populations. It may also lead to data that enables more predictive computational simulation of cellular processes, and to changes in experiments and therapeutic interventions.
